findings.title: Pipeline Consolidation Summary
findings.description: Consolidated 38 findings from 7 agents into 31 unique findings. Removed 7 duplicates. Identified 4 cross-agent conflicts.

findings.title: Top Priority Findings
findings.description: Highest priority items based on weighted scoring (user_impact×0.35 + business_value×0.30) / (effort×0.20 + risk×0.15): Primary KPI: Customer Churn Rate (score=3.37); North Star Metric: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) (score=3.265); Guardrail Metric: Net Promoter Score (NPS) (score=2.786); Implement Usage Alerts and Spending Caps (score=2.786); Competitor Landscape Overview (score=2.742)

top_priorities.title: North Star Metric: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
Implement Usage Alerts and Spending Caps

findings.title: Cross-Agent Conflict Resolution Report
findings.description: Resolved 4 conflicts between agent findings. requirements-001 vs risk-002: The implementation of a usage-based billing engine requires additional customer data collection without a clear consent mechanism, violating privacy policies.; requirements-004 vs risk-003: The grandfathering system may lead to retention of customer data beyond the 90-day limit if not properly managed.; requirements-002 vs risk-001: The development of a pricing calculator may lead to the collection of more PII than necessary, violating the data-minimal principle.

evidence.excerpt: The implementation of a usage-based billing engine requires additional customer data collection without a clear consent mechanism, violating privacy policies.
The grandfathering system may lead to retention of customer data beyond the 90-day limit if not properly managed.
The development of a pricing calculator may lead to the collection of more PII than necessary, violating the data-minimal principle.
New pricing features may not comply with WCAG standards, potentially excluding users with disabilities, which contradicts the requirement for accessibility compliance.

conflicts.description: The implementation of a usage-based billing engine requires additional customer data collection without a clear consent mechanism, violating privacy policies.
conflicts.resolution: Establish a clear consent mechanism for any new data collection related to the usage-based billing engine, ensuring compliance with privacy policies.
conflicts.reasoning: Privacy compliance is critical, and a consent mechanism will allow the feature to proceed while adhering to legal requirements.

conflicts.description: The grandfathering system may lead to retention of customer data beyond the 90-day limit if not properly managed.
conflicts.resolution: Implement strict data retention policies and automated processes to ensure customer data is deleted after the 90-day limit, even for grandfathered customers.
conflicts.reasoning: Maintaining compliance with retention policies is essential to avoid legal repercussions while allowing the grandfathering feature to be implemented.

conflicts.description: The development of a pricing calculator may lead to the collection of more PII than necessary, violating the data-minimal principle.
conflicts.resolution: Adopt a data minimization approach by only collecting essential information needed for the pricing calculator and ensuring that any PII collected is justified.
conflicts.reasoning: Balancing feature development with privacy principles is crucial to maintain user trust and comply with regulations.

conflicts.description: New pricing features may not comply with WCAG standards, potentially excluding users with disabilities, which contradicts the requirement for accessibility compliance.
conflicts.resolution: Conduct an accessibility audit during the development of new pricing features to ensure full compliance with WCAG standards.
conflicts.reasoning: Ensuring accessibility is a legal and ethical obligation that must be integrated into the development process of new features.

summary: Consolidated 38 findings into 31 unique items. Removed 7 duplicates, resolved 4 conflicts. Top priority: Primary KPI: Customer Churn Rate.
